rationale: The cited paper studies poster-side identity-signaling backfire, while the citing paper studies receiver-side effects of a mobile review cue (effort/credibility), so the overlap appears broad rather than clearly methodological or mechanistic. However, the evidence includes only a single DOI-matched reference entry and no in-text citation context, which is too sparse to safely treat as an obvious incidental mention. Therefore this pair cannot be confidently dropped at triage.
